data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The comparable universe consists of 10 peers across the sector—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at Series B stage.

Peer-median valuation is the applicable triangulation method. Across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables, the median valuation is $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. Named peers Vannevar Labs and Dream Security command $1.5B valuations; Harmattan AI sits at $1.4B; Castelion and Defense Unicorns anchor the lower end at $1.0B each. This clustering reflects investor appetite for late-stage defense tech before exit.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $1.2B. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Ocius's A$176M Australian Program of Record contract (secured March 2026) commands a premium or discount relative to peer funding stages and commercialization timelines. With only one valuation method in the analysis, sensitivity to peer selection and stage-specific adjustments remains material. Additional cost-of-capital or revenue-based methods would strengthen conviction.
